name: TOEFL 2026 Speaking — Take an Interview

criteria.name: Topic Relevance & Elaboration
criteria.description: Fully addresses the interviewer's question and stays on topic with well-developed ideas. Level 5 = on-topic and well elaborated; level 3 = on topic but elaboration limited; level 2 = minimally connected or mostly repeats language from the question.

criteria.name: Fluency & Pace
criteria.description: Good conversational pace with natural pauses. Level 5 = smooth with natural pausing; level 3 = frequent or lengthy pauses and fillers make it choppy; level 2 = halting, hard to follow.

criteria.name: Intelligibility & Pronunciation
criteria.description: Pronunciation, rhythm and intonation support meaning and require little listener effort. Lower levels: word-level pronunciation or stress/rhythm sometimes or often impede understanding.

criteria.name: Language Use
criteria.description: Range and accuracy of grammar and vocabulary that allow precise meaning. Level 5 = a range used accurately for precise meaning; lower = limited range/accuracy that restricts precision and clarity.

instructions: Official 2026 ETS 'Take an Interview' task, scored 0-5 per response (holistic; these are the named facets inside each level). This is a transcript of a spontaneous ~45-second spoken answer captured by automatic speech recognition — ignore transcription artifacts and filler words, and note that ASR cannot fully judge pronunciation, so weight topic development, fluency cues, and language use most. Be encouraging but honest. The 0-5 task scores feed the 1.0-6.0 Speaking section band.
